comment: Single source of truth for all tunable model parameters. Edit this file to change model behavior without touching code.

farmer_defaults.comment_fixed_cost: Annual fixed operating cost per hectare (land rent + machinery depreciation + admin/overhead), charged monthly as area*cost/12 regardless of activity. This is the margin pressure that makes insolvency, exit and entry meaningful; sowing_cost_per_ha alone leaves margins permanently fat. 0 disables.

farmer_area_and_storage.comment: Random ranges for newly generated farmers (farm-size range is now per climate zone — see climate_zones)

climate_zones.comment: Agro-climatic zones (must match the ClimateZone enum in geo.py). yield_multiplier scales EVERY crop's base yield in that zone (south best, north worst — season length, heat, moisture). cost_multiplier scales sowing + fixed costs (land rent, inputs, labour) — lower in Siberia/north, so low-yield zones stay viable on cheaper land instead of all going bankrupt; the south is most profitable but not the only viable zone. farmer_area_ha_range is the typical farm-size range there.

farmer_behavior.comment: Algorithm constants governing farmer decision logic
farmer_behavior.comment_demand_premium: Max fractional uplift applied to clearing price when buyer demand exceeds supply (fill_rate=0 → price * (1 + premium)). Provides the upward price signal that balances the downward storage-discount pressure and restores cobweb oscillation.
farmer_behavior.comment_surplus_bargaining: Seller's share of the buyer surplus in the search market's surplus-split pricing. Each trade executes at ask + this * max(0, (buyer_valuation - transport) - ask), so the demand side lifts the price toward (but never above) the buyer's willingness-to-pay even without scarcity. 0 = buyer pays the seller's ask (old behaviour); 1 = buyer pays its full valuation. NOTE: empirically the dominant driver of within-year price oscillation (each trade is repriced toward the matched buyer's valuation, and monthly buyer/seller re-pairing injects noise), so lowered from 0.5 to 0.35 to damp it.
farmer_behavior.comment_ema: EMA smoothing for monthly cash-flow profitability signal (half-life ~8 months)
farmer_behavior.comment_sale_expectation: Adaptation α for the TRADING (reservation) price belief that prices monthly sale offers, kept SEPARATE from price_adaptation_speed (the PLANTING belief) so the two timescales tune independently. Kept responsive on purpose: a laggy ask worsens within-year oscillation. The dominant within-year driver is surplus_bargaining_power, not this.
farmer_behavior.comment_rotation: (legacy) Fraction of sibling-group area blocked by the old rotation constraint; superseded by per-crop max_area_share in crops.json
farmer_behavior.comment_acreage_inertia: Nerlovian partial adjustment on ACREAGE: a farm's new sowing mix = inertia*last_year + (1-inertia)*margin_target. Captures sunk equipment, agronomic familiarity and contracts that make last year's mix sticky. 0 = jump to the margin-optimal mix each year (volatile); ~0.5-0.7 = realistic gradual reallocation. Equilibrium mix is set by margins within each crop's max_area_share cap.
farmer_behavior.comment_fill_pressure: Extra sell-fraction added per unit fill above storage_fill_threshold
farmer_behavior.comment_fill_threshold: Fill ratio above which storage pressure increases sell fraction
farmer_behavior.comment_max_discount: Maximum price discount applied when storage is very full
farmer_behavior.comment_discount_activation: Fill ratio above which price discount begins to apply
farmer_behavior.comment_discount_ramp: Slope of discount vs (fill_ratio - activation_threshold). Softened (was 0.6) to damp the within-year storage-discount feedback swing.
farmer_behavior.comment_low_fill: Sell-fraction damping when storage fill is below threshold

buyer_behavior.comment: Algorithm constants governing buyer decision logic
buyer_behavior.comment_markup: Markup over observed market price used as target when no output_price is configured (e.g. traders)
buyer_behavior.comment_resale: Markup over market price at which traders book resale revenue in consume()
buyer_behavior.comment_cost_ema: EMA smoothing for tracking actual price paid vs target (demand contraction)
buyer_behavior.comment_inventory: Desired raw-material cover in months of consumption; buyers purchase toward this level (capped by storage capacity) and consume one month's worth, so they carry a buffer instead of running at zero stock

buyer_demand_contraction.comment: When enabled, buyers reduce consumption if they pay too much for too long and recover when prices fall back
buyer_demand_contraction.comment_threshold: Contraction: price EMA must exceed target by >N% to count as an overpayment month
buyer_demand_contraction.comment_months: Contraction: triggers after N consecutive overpayment months
buyer_demand_contraction.comment_reduction: Contraction: reduce monthly_consumption by this fraction per trigger
buyer_demand_contraction.comment_expansion_threshold: Expansion: price EMA must be at least N% BELOW target to count as an underpayment month
buyer_demand_contraction.comment_expansion_months: Expansion: triggers after N consecutive underpayment months
buyer_demand_contraction.comment_expansion_recovery: Expansion: recover this fraction of the gap to baseline consumption per trigger (never exceeds original baseline)

buyer_profiles.comment: Crops here are MARKET GOODS (see crops.json:market_good) — buyers trade fungible commodities (wheat), not agronomic varieties (winter/spring wheat). Mills/processors derive their input target price from output_prices net of processing_margin.

government_defaults.comment: Max share of a region's traded volume the government moves per intervention per month
government_defaults.comment_direct_tax: Flat direct tax withheld from the seller's revenue on every deal (e.g. 6% ≈ УСН «доходы»)

world_config.comment_window: Months of price/volume history retained and exposed to agents
world_config.comment_demand_center: Reference point (Samara/Saratov corridor) for transport-discount on national avg price

scenario_defaults.comment_farmer_jitter: Max lat/lon offset in degrees when placing farmers within their region
scenario_defaults.comment_buyer_jitter: Max lat/lon offset in degrees when placing buyers within their region
scenario_defaults.comment_farmer_crops: Range for how many crop types each generated farmer grows (sampled from crops suitable for its zone; a southern/temperate mix will typically include both winter and spring crops, giving two harvests/year)
scenario_defaults.comment_staple: Market goods almost every grain farm grows: at least one zone-suitable variety of each is forced into every farm's crop list, so wheat is near-universal (as in reality) and acts as the high-cap residual filler in the planting allocation
scenario_defaults.comment_buyer_crops: Range for how many crop types each generated buyer handles
scenario_defaults.comment_entry_baseline: Entry probability for a region with no farmers (fraction of max_rate)
scenario_defaults.comment_max_debt_ha: RUB/ha multiplier used to set per-farmer max_debt floor
scenario_defaults.comment_farmer_cash: Initial cash (RUB/ha) for hand-placed extra_farmers without an explicit cash field
scenario_defaults.comment_buyer_cash: Initial cash (RUB) for hand-placed extra_buyers without an explicit cash field
scenario_defaults.comment_scale_ref: Farmer count at which exporter capacities in border_points.json are at real-world scale

farm_lifecycle.comment_closure: Consecutive insolvent months before a farm is automatically closed
farm_lifecycle.comment_entry_rate: Maximum annual probability of a new farm entering a region
farm_lifecycle.comment_entry_profit: RUB/ha/month EMA at which regional entry probability reaches farm_entry_rate_max
farm_lifecycle.comment_farmer_debt: RUB — farmer suspends planting beyond this debt
farm_lifecycle.comment_buyer_debt: RUB — buyer suspends purchases beyond this debt
farm_lifecycle.comment_buyer_closure: Consecutive insolvent months before a buyer (processor/trader) is automatically closed
farm_lifecycle.comment_buyer_entry_rate: Maximum annual probability of a new buyer entering a populated region
farm_lifecycle.comment_buyer_entry_profit: RUB/month cash-EMA at which regional buyer entry probability reaches buyer_entry_rate_max

weather.comment: Variance decomposition of the multiplicative yield shock into shared national + regional factors plus an idiosyncratic farm draw. Weights must sum to 1; each farm's marginal yield volatility stays equal to the crop's yield_volatility — only the CORRELATION across farms changes. Raising the national/regional weights produces synchronised good/bad harvest years (real source of price cycles). All-idiosyncratic (0/0/1) reproduces the old independent-draw behaviour.

world_dynamics.comment: Exogenous global-market state. The deterministic seasonal world_prices series is scaled each month by a global commodity shock and the RUB/USD rate, both mean-reverting AR(1) processes in log space. Set volatilities to 0 for a purely deterministic world-price feed.
world_dynamics.comment_fx_base: Reference RUB/USD the base world_prices series is quoted at
world_dynamics.comment_fx_vol: Monthly stdev of log RUB/USD changes (0 = fixed FX)
world_dynamics.comment_fx_rev: AR(1) pull of the FX rate back toward fx_base per month
world_dynamics.comment_wp_vol: Monthly stdev of the log global commodity-price shock (0 = deterministic)
world_dynamics.comment_wp_rev: AR(1) pull of the commodity shock back toward 1.0 per month

world_prices.comment: Base FOB price (RUB/ton), seasonal amplitude (fraction of base), and peak month (1-12) per MARKET GOOD (see crops.json:market_good — one price per traded commodity, shared by its winter and spring varieties).
